characters.description: Captain of the Tennyson, a human vessel operating under Ornu rule. He quietly coordinates with Bina Chakravarti beyond Imperial surveillance while deciding how to investigate an ancient transmission without exposing his crew to punishment.
characters.name: Bill Henderson

characters.description: The Tennyson's experienced executive officer and Bill Henderson's long-serving second-in-command. She helps trace the unexplained signal and uses a subtle sign language with Bill to communicate beyond Imperial surveillance.
characters.name: Bina Chakravarti

characters.description: The Tennyson's communications officer, who detects a scrambled transmission that repeats aboard the ship.
characters.name: Sally Longfield

characters.description: A Marine serving with Alden Stone's team aboard the Tennyson.
characters.name: Funnybone

characters.description: A hostile Ornu official who observes the Tennyson's human crew on behalf of the Imperium.
characters.name: The Tennyson's Ornu monitor

characters.description: Captain of the Tennyson's seven-person Marine team. He monitors a discouraged crew, tries to prevent unrest, and joins the ancient-signal investigation while Imperial kill switches constrain the command staff.
characters.name: Alden Stone

characters.description: An Ornu consul on Earth and the uncle of the Tennyson's monitor. His authority gives his nephew influence over the investigation into the ancient transmission.
characters.name: Philo

characters.description: A new Marine private on Stone's team, also known as Newby. She has already witnessed the Imperium impose summary punishment on her former captain.
characters.name: Jen Lambert

characters.description: The newest Marine on Stone's team. He is literal-minded and inexperienced, and joins the team's assignment to monitor crew morale.
characters.name: Porker

characters.description: A Marine serving with Alden Stone's team aboard the Tennyson.
characters.name: Splat

characters.description: A member of Stone's Marine team assigned to watch crew morale as the ancient signal draws the Tennyson into danger.
characters.name: Termite

characters.description: The Tennyson's weapons officer, involved as the crew traces the ancient transmission to an object near an asteroid.
characters.name: Hans Norder

characters.description: The Tennyson's navigation officer. He triangulates the ancient transmission to a metallic object orbiting an asteroid near a gas giant.
characters.name: James Riding

characters.description: Lance Corporal Rhonda Penny, an experienced Marine known as Guns. During the drone attack, a failed magnetic boot seal leaves her drifting away from the Tennyson with communications apparently disabled.
characters.name: Guns

characters.description: Stone's technical Marine specialist, carrying the team's equipment and handling entry systems. Tubes forces open the sealed outer door of the artificial asteroid installation.
characters.name: Tubes

characters.description: An ancient warship named by the recovered transmitter. Its message declares that it has awakened and is waiting for a party it judges worthy, while warning of an approaching soulless threat.
characters.name: Omega

characters.description: Bill Henderson's former friend and a senior UN military official. Although human military institutions now advise Ornu controllers, he can still influence recommendations concerning the Tennyson and the wider ancient-technology mystery.
characters.name: Stuart Garland

characters.description: A private in Alden Stone's Marine team, previously known to the crew as Termite.
characters.name: Jorge Gonzalez

characters.description: A sergeant in Alden Stone's Marine team, previously known to the crew as Funnybone. During the dreadnought boarding, he acts quickly to save another Marine from being pulled into space.
characters.name: Sean Piker

characters.description: A weapons specialist who takes control of the missile station aboard a captured enemy dreadnought while Bina directs its defense.
characters.name: Felicia Keating

characters.description: James Riding's sister. Her reported killing by an Ornu during shore leave leaves her brother grieving and intent on retaliation.
characters.name: Tanya Riding

characters.description: Bill Henderson's mother, whom he visits during shore leave. She has received no recent word about her husband Gordon, who remains imprisoned in a helium mine.
characters.name: Miriam Henderson

characters.description: Bill Henderson's father, imprisoned and forced to work in a helium mine, with no recent news reaching his family.
characters.name: Gordon Henderson

characters.description: The hostile Ornu monitor assigned to the Tennyson and Philo's nephew. Ambitious and suspicious of the human crew, he hopes the ancient discovery will advance his standing.
characters.name: Nonas

characters.description: An Ornu Imperator directing efforts to exploit Omega. He removes Stone from the Tennyson and threatens the Marine team to force Stone's cooperation with the ancient ship.
characters.name: Pertinax

characters.description: An Ornu consul serving under Pertinax during the effort to gain control of Omega.
characters.name: Galleus

characters.description: A figure who meets Bill inside Omega's simulated nineteenth-century London and introduces himself as the ship's representative. Val offers Bill a limited channel to Omega if he can prove trustworthy.
characters.name: Val

characters.description: Pertinax's discreet analyst. She finds evidence that Omega conducts private simulations which the Ornu can neither hear nor activate.
characters.name: Cecilia

characters.description: A second interface through which Omega communicates, distinct from Val. Tobias confronts Stone with the threatened capital world and tests whether he will reject Ornu authority.
characters.name: Tobias

characters.description: Leader of a newly conquered vassal group that offers to join the human revolt. He delivers Galleus captive as proof that his people are committed to opposing Ornu control.
characters.name: Tull

characters.description: Director of a Confederation refueling and alloy-processing station. He receives Bill's explanation of the capital-world attack and considers a voluntary visit to Omega rather than submitting to coercion.
characters.name: Kip

characters.description: A confident allied technical specialist assigned to Stone's Geneva team. She is to enter the Ornu consulate with Stone while the rest of the team provides cover.
characters.name: Raina
